danger_signals.visual_form: Warsaw Pact tank and vehicle sprites on tactical map and 3D viewport
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player platoon; engagement required or evasion necessary

danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming fire trajectory or impact visual on terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Stationary friendly positions under enemy artillery bombardment; repositioning required

danger_signals.visual_form: Density of enemy unit markers on tactical map exceeding friendly force count
danger_signals.meaning: Defensive scenario: NATO must use technological advantage to stem 'wave' of Warsaw Pact vehicles

danger_signals.visual_form: Tank model standing above terrain skyline in 3D view (not hull-down)
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability to anti-tank fire; tactically weak positioning

safety_signals.visual_form: Tank turret visible above terrain ridge with hull obscured
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive advantage; reduced exposed target profile

safety_signals.visual_form: Recon helicopters, attack helicopters, M2 Bradley IFVs, MLRS, or M60 Patton tanks visible on map/viewport
safety_signals.meaning: Combat force multiplier; enhanced survivability and firepower

safety_signals.visual_form: Waypoint marker on tactical map aligned with platoon position
safety_signals.meaning: Mission progress secured; temporary strategic success

safety_signals.visual_form: Surviving crew member rank advancement notification between battles
safety_signals.meaning: Team competency growth; morale and combat effectiveness improvement

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: M1 Abrams tank insignia; U.S. military markings
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Rhine crossing campaign zone (Central Europe); defensive then offensive positions

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Soviet military vehicle markings; numerical force wave depiction
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Tank and vehicle sprite designs distinct from NATO equipment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Numerically superior forces attacking across Central European terrain; artillery support coordinated with main assault

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tactical map shows NATO forces in prepared positions with Warsaw Pact 'wave' approaching from east
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must use technologically superior M1 Abrams, Bradley IFVs, and support units to hold territory against numerical superiority

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Objectives shift from holding ground to advancing waypoints westward
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Campaign scenarios gradually transition to offensive operations with forward objective markers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Artillery strike indicators on map; impact visuals on terrain and unit positions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Friendly stationary units suffer sustained damage; repositioning becomes critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Recon helicopter or support unit available on map; viewpoint can switch to external unit perspective
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains 'recon' visibility; approximates tank commander standing on hull for better battlefield awareness

environmental_storytelling.example: Hull-down positioning mechanic communicates that 1989 tank warfare hinges on landscape mastery; exposed tanks on open ground are death traps
Rhine crossing campaign setup establishes Cold War scenario context; defensive-to-offensive arc mirrors real military doctrine of NATO holding then counter-attacking
Infantry disembarking from M2 Bradley to deploy M47 Dragon anti-tank launchers visually demonstrates combined-arms doctrine; leaving them stationary invites enemy retaliation
Named or tracked crew members gaining rank between battles creates narrative continuity; player incentivizes keeping specific personnel alive

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Reduced visual profile; turret-only engagement possible; higher survivability against incoming fire
Full silhouette visible; high vulnerability to anti-tank fire; tactical weakness communicated through model visibility
Massed vehicle advancement visible on tactical map; numerically overwhelming visual representation; requires technological NATO advantage to counter
Repositionable via external viewpoint camera; provides reconnaissance and fire support; presence reduces friendly force vulnerability
Strike indicators on map; impact animations on terrain; forces repositioning of stationary friendly units or artillery countermeasures

notes: M1 Tank Platoon (1989) uses a dual-perspective manifestation system: tactical map for strategy/world-state overview and first/third-person tank views for immersive danger/safety assessment. The game communicates Cold War NATO vs. Warsaw Pact political conflict through campaign narrative arc (defensive Rhine crossing → offensive operations) and force composition disparities. Terrain, positioning, and support unit availability are the primary visual language for communicating tactical advantage/disadvantage. Crew survival mechanics create persistent entity identification, reinforcing individual tank-commander narrative. The game lacks explicit faction color-coding in available source material but distinguishes sides through vehicle model design and campaign geography.
sources_used: wikipedia: M1 Tank Platoon article (gameplay section, campaign description, vehicle mechanics)
